Formal Truth Grid A

Four agents labeled 1, 2, 3, and 4 each make the statements listed below.
Exactly one agent is the universal liar: every statement made by that agent is false.
The other three agents make only true statements.
Exactly one agent is guilty.
The liar and the guilty agent are distinct.

Notation:
Li: agent i is the universal liar.
Ti: agent i is fully truthful.
Gi: agent i is guilty.
Pi: agent i was present at the event.
S(a,b): statement b made by agent a is true.

Statements:
Agent 1:
  (1) (L2 -> not G3)
  (2) (P4 -> not L2)
  (3) not S(3,2)
  (4) not G1
Agent 2:
  (1) G2
  (2) P2
  (3) L3
  (4) (P4 -> not L1)
Agent 3:
  (1) L2
  (2) not P4
  (3) (P2 <-> L4)
  (4) (G2 -> L4)
Agent 4:
  (1) not G4
  (2) P4
  (3) (L1 <-> G3)
  (4) (L1 -> G2)

Determine the guilty agent. Return a single digit 1-4.

Solution.
Enumerate all assignments of the unique liar, the unique guilty agent, the presence bits P1..P4,
and any referenced truth-value variables until every statement and every truth-reference is evaluated consistently.
Keep only the worlds in which one speaker has all false statements and the other three speakers have all true statements.
This leaves 4 consistent worlds.
All surviving worlds force agent 3 to be the universal liar.
Every referenced S(a,b) value is simultaneously checked against the truth value of statement (a,b).
All surviving worlds agree that agent 2 is guilty.
Therefore the correct answer is 2.

2
